title: Fleet Management: Onboard an OpenTelemetry Collector

blocks.content: In this guide, you'll deploy an OpenTelemetry Collector with the OpAMP Supervisor and connect it to Fleet Management. You'll then create a self-monitoring pipeline and verify your collector appears in the inventory.
You'll connect to a terminal session where you'll install and run the OpenTelemetry Collector.
Connect to a terminal session. This is where you'll run installation and configuration commands.
Verify the terminal is working by running a quick command.
Your terminal is ready for the next steps.

blocks.title: Create an access policy and token

blocks.content: You'll create a Grafana Cloud access policy with the scopes needed for Fleet Management and telemetry ingestion.
Navigate to **Access Policies** in Grafana Cloud.
Click **Create access policy**.
Configure the access policy. Give it a name, add your stack to the realms, and add the required scopes.

steps.description: Enter a name for the access policy.
Add your stack to the realms list.
Click **Create** to save the access policy.

blocks.content: Now create a token for this access policy. Click **Add token**.
Copy the token and save it somewhere safe. You'll need it to configure the OpAMP Supervisor.
You now have an access policy token with the required scopes.

blocks.title: Install OpenTelemetry Collector and OpAMP Supervisor

blocks.content: You'll install the OpenTelemetry Collector Contrib distribution and the OpAMP Supervisor.

blocks.command: sudo apt-get update && sudo apt-get install -y otelcol-contrib
blocks.content: Install the OpenTelemetry Collector Contrib distribution.
Install the OpAMP Supervisor.
Both the OpenTelemetry Collector and OpAMP Supervisor are now installed.
You'll gather connection details from Fleet Management and configure the OpAMP Supervisor to connect your collector.
Open **Fleet Management** from the main menu.
Select the **API** tab to find your connection details.
Note the **Fleet Management URL** and **Instance ID** shown in this section. You'll use these in the next steps.

You need to set the following environment variables before starting the supervisor:

- `GCLOUD_FM_URL` — Fleet Management URL (from the API tab)
- `GCLOUD_OTLP_URL` — OTLP gateway URL (from Grafana Cloud Portal > Your stacks > OpenTelemetry)
- `GCLOUD_INSTANCE_ID` — your Grafana Cloud instance ID (from the API tab)
- `GCLOUD_RW_API_KEY` — the access policy token you created earlier
- `GCLOUD_BASIC_AUTH` — base64-encoded `instance_id:token`
Expand the terminal panel.

blocks.command:
export GCLOUD_FM_URL="<FLEET_MANAGEMENT_URL>"
export GCLOUD_OTLP_URL="<OTLP_GATEWAY_URL>"
export GCLOUD_INSTANCE_ID="<INSTANCE_ID>"
export GCLOUD_RW_API_KEY="<ACCESS_POLICY_TOKEN>"
export GCLOUD_BASIC_AUTH=$(echo "${GCLOUD_INSTANCE_ID}:${GCLOUD_RW_API_KEY}" | openssl base64 -A)
blocks.content: Set the environment variables. Replace the placeholder values with your actual connection details and token.

blocks.command:
cat > supervisor-config.yaml << 'EOF'
server:
  endpoint: "${env:GCLOUD_FM_URL}/v1/opamp"
  headers:
    Authorization: "Basic ${env:GCLOUD_BASIC_AUTH}"

capabilities:
  accepts_remote_config: true
  reports_remote_config: true

agent:
  executable: /usr/bin/otelcol-contrib
  description:
    identifying_attributes:
      collector_name: my-otel-collector
    non_identifying_attributes:
      environment: "dev"
  args: [--feature-gates, service.AllowNoPipelines]

storage:
  directory: /var/lib/otelcol/supervisor
EOF
blocks.content: Create the supervisor configuration file.
The OpAMP Supervisor is now configured to connect to your Fleet Management instance.
You'll start the OpAMP Supervisor, which launches and manages the OpenTelemetry Collector.
Start the OpAMP Supervisor. It will launch the OpenTelemetry Collector and connect to Fleet Management.
The supervisor is now running and managing your OpenTelemetry Collector.
You'll create a remote configuration pipeline that sends the collector's internal metrics, logs, and traces to Grafana Cloud. This enables health monitoring in Fleet Management.
Navigate to **Fleet Management**.
Select the **Remote Configuration** tab.
Click **Create configuration pipeline**.
Select the collector type and configuration template.

steps.description: Select the platform where your collector is running.

blocks.content: Review the generated pipeline name and YAML configuration. Click **Test configuration pipeline** to validate the syntax.
Once the test passes, click **Next**.
Assign matching attributes to roll the configuration out to your collectors.

steps.description: Set the matcher to regex match.
Set the value to match all collectors.

blocks.content: Click **Save** and confirm to save the pipeline.
Find your pipeline in the list and click **Activate** to enable it.
Your self-monitoring pipeline is now active and will push collector health data to Grafana Cloud.
You'll confirm the collector appears in the Fleet Management inventory with a healthy status.
Navigate to the **Fleet Inventory**.
Select the **Inventory** tab.
Your OpenTelemetry Collector should now appear in the list.

Verify the following:

- **Status** is green
- **Collector ID** is present
- **Version**, **OS**, and **Attributes** are populated
Click your collector to view its health, logs, attributes, and merged configuration.
blocks.tooltip: Shows collector details including health metrics and active configuration

blocks.content: You've confirmed your collector is connected and reporting to Fleet Management.
Your OpenTelemetry Collector is now connected to Fleet Management via the OpAMP Supervisor. From here you can push remote configurations, monitor collector health, and manage your fleet at scale.
